items.prompt: A differential equation is an equation that involves:
items.answer: derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variable

expected.correct: derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variable
options: derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variable
only the dependent and independent variables, no derivatives

items.prompt: The order of the differential equation whose highest order derivative is the second derivative y'' is:
The degree of a differential equation is defined only when the equation is:
items.answer: a polynomial equation in the derivatives

expected.correct: a polynomial equation in the derivatives
options: a polynomial equation in the derivatives

items.prompt: The solution of a differential equation that contains arbitrary constants is called the:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Sec 9.3 general solution

items.prompt: How many arbitrary constants are in the particular solution of a third order differential equation?
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Exercise 9.3 Q12 (particular solution has 0 constants)

items.prompt: A first order equation is of variables-separable type when dy/dx can be written as:

options: a homogeneous function of degree zero

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Sec 9.4.1 variables separable

items.prompt: The function F(x, y) = x^2 + xy is a homogeneous function of degree:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Sec 9.4.2 homogeneous function (degree 2)

items.prompt: To solve a homogeneous differential equation dy/dx = F(x, y) (F of degree zero), the standard substitution is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Sec 9.4.2 substitution y = vx

items.prompt: The standard form of a first order linear differential equation is:
items.answer: dy/dx + Py = Q, with P, Q functions of x only

expected.correct: dy/dx + Py = Q, with P, Q functions of x only
options: dy/dx + Py = Q, with P, Q functions of x only

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Sec 9.4.3 linear DE

items.prompt: For dy/dx + Py = Q, the integrating factor is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Sec 9.4.3 integrating factor

items.prompt: After finding the integrating factor (I.F.) of dy/dx + Py = Q, the solution is given by:
items.answer: y(I.F.) = integral of (Q x I.F.) dx + C

expected.correct: y(I.F.) = integral of (Q x I.F.) dx + C
options: y(I.F.) = integral of (Q x I.F.) dx + C
y = integral of Q dx + C
y(I.F.) = integral of P dx + C

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - mathematics-ch09 Sec 9.4.3 solution steps

items.prompt: List the steps to solve a first order linear differential equation of the form dy/dx + Py = Q.

expected.rubric: (1) Write the equation in standard form dy/dx + Py = Q and identify P and Q (functions of x only). (2) Find the integrating factor I.F. = e^(integral of P dx). (3) Write the solution as y(I.F.) = integral of (Q times I.F.) dx + C, and apply any given condition to find a particular solution.
markPoints: Write in standard form dy/dx + Py = Q and identify P, Q
Compute the integrating factor I.F. = e^(integral of P dx)
Solution: y(I.F.) = integral of (Q x I.F.) dx + C
